title: Displaying Errors in Dynamic Server Pages
breadcrumbs: Displaying Errors in Dynamic Server Pages
text: Displaying Errors in Dynamic Server Pages Field syntax and validation errors can be displayed in dynamic server pages by inserting a bound error element in the layout. When a syntax error occurs in a field that is rendered as an HTML 5 control, a red border is displayed around the field, but no error message is shown. To display an error message for a field: In the HTML layout of the component, insert an HTML element such as span (or label or div ) where you want the error message to be displayed. Bind this element to the the data field by adding an id attribute in the format : uflderror: FIELD.ENTITY.MODEL . For example, For every field with browser-side syntax enabled, you could have three bound elements in the layout, one for the field, one for the field label, and one for the error. For example: Copy To style the error message, you can use CSS to style the -uflderror-in-error- class attribute. For example: Explain this code CSS for error styling Copy Other classes are available for styling the field in error. When there is an element bound to uflderror in the layout, and a syntax error is encountered, the error message is displayed in the bound element. For example, in the following illustration Order Number is a Numeric field but the user has entered a character. When the user tabs to the next field, the syntax check is performed and an error displayed: Figure 1. Bound Syntax Error Related Links Syntax Checking in Dynamic Server Pages Uniface always performs server-side syntax checks when data in a dynamic server page (DSP) is validated or stored. It is also possible to perform browser-side syntax checks, so that errors are flagged before incorrect data is submitted to the server. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
